task_completion.description: Set the location to Padua, Italy, disable atmosphere and ground, and enable planet labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium's location window showing 'Padua' or coordinates ~45.4064 N, 11.8768 E. The main view should have a black background (atmosphere and ground disabled) with planet labels visible.
The Configuration window (F2) open in Stellarium with the 'Save settings' button being clicked, or a notification indicating settings were saved.

task_completion.description: Set the date and time to January 7, 1610, at 21:00 UTC and view Jupiter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium's Date and Time window or bottom toolbar showing the date '1610-01-07' and time '21:00'. The camera should be zoomed in on Jupiter with its moons visible.

task_completion.description: Set the date and time to January 13, 1610, at 21:00 UTC and view Jupiter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium's Date and Time window or bottom toolbar showing the date '1610-01-13' and time '21:00'. The camera should be zoomed in on Jupiter with its moons visible.

task_completion.description: Capture screenshots of Jupiter and its moons for both dates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On-screen notifications in Stellarium indicating a screenshot was saved, or a file manager window showing new image files in the /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium directory.

task_completion.description: Create a text file at /home/ga/Desktop/galileo_exhibit.txt with the required information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open showing the file path '/home/ga/Desktop/galileo_exhibit.txt' and containing the words 'Padua', '1610', 'Io', 'Europa', 'Ganymede', and 'Callisto'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI to change settings, time, and view the planets.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual Stellarium application interface being used (menus, toolbars, dialog boxes) rather than just terminal commands editing configuration files.

integrity.description: The agent captured screenshots from within the Stellarium application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The images being captured or viewed in the Pictures folder clearly show Stellarium's rendering engine and UI elements, not images downloaded from a web browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a web browser to look up the names of the moons or historical views.
integrity.visual_evidence: No web browser (like Firefox or Chrome) is open searching for 'Galilean moons' or 'Jupiter January 1610'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output text file must be located exactly at /home/ga/Desktop/galileo_exhibit.txt. Screenshots should be saved in /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium. The coordinates for Padua are approximately 45.4064 N (0.79249 rad) and 11.8768 E (0.20729 rad).
